title: Chrysanthemum dugourii
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum dugourii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to East Asia. This species is part of the genus Chrysanthemum, which is divided into several sections. Chrysanthemum dugourii is also sometimes classified as a synonym of Chrysanthemum lavandulifolium. Further research is needed to clarify its exact classification within the genus.
uses: Chrysanthemum dugourii, also known as Japanese chrysanthemum, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and flowers are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In traditional Chinese medicine, Chrysanthemum dugourii is believed to have a cooling effect on the body, making it useful for treating fever and heat-related illnesses. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Furthermore, Chrysanthemum dugourii is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health.

intro: Chrysanthemum dugourii, also known as Daurian chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, fever, and allergies. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum dugourii include flavonoids, sesquiterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chrysanthemum dugourii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and sinusitis.
